prompt: I want to run simulation-based inference for image-based Bayesian denoising. You need to download the Fashion MNIST dataset using keras (https://keras.io/api/datasets/fashion_mnist/). Treat the clean images as targets ('parameters') and the corresponding blurry images (you need to apply Gaussian blur) as conditions ('data'). Set up a BayesFlow workflow with proper handling of images as targets. Use a small subset of the train data as validation data.
assertions: Defines a BasicWorkflow without an explicit simulator
Uses the clean images as inference_variables and the noisy images as inference_conditions
Does NOT flatten any of the images
Does NOT use a summary network (SetTransformer, DeepSet, ConvolutionalNetwork, etc.)
Uses a diffusion model or flow matching with a UNet architecture as a subnet
Uses the fit_offline method of a BasicWorkflow
Uses bf.Adapter() with an explicit adapter chain (not just naming kwargs)
Ensures conditioning tensor is spatially concatenable with the image target — same (B, H, W, C/D) shape
Passes validation_data= to the fit_offline call
Saves history.history as JSON and runs inspect_training or equivalent convergence check
Does NOT run scripts/check_diagnostics.py or conventional in-silico diagnostics for low-dimensional problems
Creates and inspects a few posterior clean images for a few test examples (visual sample grid)
